# Explore some interesting interactions between two voters and a witness
# (i.e. in 3x configuration).

setup
v1: [a1]
v2: [a1]
w
----
v1: [a1] term=a
v2: [a1] term=a

# v1 wins election. Witness vote is dropped to hit an interesting case in
# which the witness finds out about the leader later. Letting it vote would
# work too and is unit tested.
campaign v1 drop=(w)
----
v1 campaigns at term b
v1: yes (self)
v2:
  - term advance a -> b
  - granted vote for v1 at term b
  yes (term a->b, voted v1)
w: (dropped)
outcome: won

# v2 goes down.
set-avail down=(v2)
----
ok

# v1 proposes an entry.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b

# Since v2 is down, there's no way to commit unless v2 returns or the witness
# comes into play.
append v1
----
v1 appends through b3
v2: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b

# v1 engages the witness using its last index. The witness promises to "ack" the
# log up to this point, and also any future entries in the term until
# disengagement. Because (v1+witness) is a quorum, v1 commits its log.
engage v1
----
w:
  - term advance 0 -> b (leader v1)
  - engaged at b3
  ok (term 0->b, voted v1, hi 0->b3, engaged)
w:  vote=(b, v1) acked=(b3, engaged)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b committed=b3 leader

# v2 comes back, but v1 goes down. We are now unavailable: the witness has the
# more up to date (fictitious) log, so it won't vote for v2, but it also can't
# campaign. Also, if v1 were lost permanently (not just unavailable), we'd have
# lost durability. Effectively, (v1+witness) has the durability of a single-voter
# group.
set-avail up=(v2) down=(v1)
----
ok

# Confirm that witness won't vote for v2. The witness has to assume that v1 has
# more up to date log entries from the engaged term, and v2's log is not
# obviously up to date with whatever the engaged term's entries might be.
prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
w:
  - engaged: effectiveHi inflated to b∞
  - rejected vote for v2: log not up to date (effectiveHi b∞ > lm a1)
  no
outcome: lost

# v1 comes back up, v2 goes back down.
set-avail up=(v1) down=(v2)
----
ok

# v1 could continue using the witness (nothing in the pair's state has changed
# since it last did) but for extra spice, let's say it power cycled and lost
# leadership. This creates the interesting situation in which v1 re-campaigns,
# and needs a vote from the witness but the witness is still engaged at the
# prior term. It typically wouldn't be able to vote here, but it is able to
# here because it remembers that the candidate is the leader for the engaged
# term, which proves that its log is up to date with anything the witness may
# have helped synthesize acks for.
#
# Note that the witness disengages in the new term, preserving a high water mark
# of b∞ (anything in term b may have been committed with the witness's help).
# The leader also doesn't consider the witness engaged. It's a new term.
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term c
v1: yes (self)
v2: (dropped)
w:
  - engaged: effectiveHi inflated to b∞
  - engaged leader v1 re-campaigning: skipping log check
  - granted vote for v1 at term c, hi carried as b∞
  yes (term b->c, hi b3->b∞, released)
outcome: won

# Nobody to append to, i.e. this is a no-op.
append v1
----
v1 appends through c4
v2: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3 c4] term=c committed=b3 leader
v2: [a1] term=b

# v2 comes back, v1 goes down again. The witness disengaged when it voted for
# v1 at term c, but preserved a high water mark of b∞ — reflecting that it may
# have helped commit any entry in term b. v2's log (term a) can't prove it
# covers what the witness helped commit, so the prevote fails.
set-avail up=(v2) down=(v1)
----
ok

prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
w:
  - rejected vote for v2: candidate term c <= vote term c (votedFor=v1)
  no
outcome: lost

set-avail up=(v1)
----
ok

# v1 proposes and replicates to v2, committing via the traditional v1+v2
# quorum — no witness engagement needed.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=c committed=b3 leader
v2: [a1] term=b

append v1
----
v1 appends through c5
v2:
  - term advance b -> c (leader v1)
  - accepted append through c5 (ok)
  ok (term b->c, log=[a1 | b2 b3 c4 c5])
v1: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=c committed=c5 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3 c4 c5] term=c
